data.content:
222
The input text is a standalone number, "222".

副本积分
副本积分是GVG副本中用于记录对战双方联盟当前得分的数据，通过蓝、红颜色文本框在UI界面上区分显示。

GVG副本UI界面
GVG副本UI界面是在玩家主界面顶部中央增加的显示组件，用于展示副本积分和有效时间倒计时。

副本战报
副本战报是GVG副本中争夺建筑战斗的记录，会发送给相关玩家并缓存在建筑中供双方查看，格式与沙盘战报相同。

GVG副本
GVG副本是一个游戏内活动，具有开启和关闭的有效时间，玩家在副本内进行战斗，结束时根据积分结算奖励。

GVG活动界面
GVG活动界面是玩家进入或查看GVG副本状态的交互界面，在副本结束后显示活动结束状态。

系统邮件
系统邮件是GVG副本奖励的发放方式，所有类型的奖励都通过此途径发送给玩家。

副本有效时间
副本有效时间是GVG副本从开启到关闭的预设时长，在副本中以倒计时方式显示，归零时所有玩家被移出副本。

GVG副本奖励
GVG副本奖励是副本活动结束后系统发放给参赛联盟成员的奖励，分为获胜方和战败方两大类，每类包含参战与未参战成员奖励。

GVG观战功能
GVG观战功能允许未报名的玩家在副本开启后进入战场观看战斗，可以拖动和缩放场景，但不支持查看战报详情。

黑板功能
黑板功能是关卡编辑器的一部分，用于加载指定的玩法配置表，管理多个触发器之间的数据，其数据在编辑器界面中可见并可选择。

关卡编辑器
关卡编辑器是用于设计游戏关卡的工具，包含黑板功能，可以加载外部配置表数据供触发器和条件使用。

玩法配置表
玩法配置表是存储游戏玩法规则和参数的数据表格，可以被关卡编辑器的黑板功能加载和使用。

编辑器配置文件
编辑器配置文件是关卡编辑器的设置文件，用于定义编辑器各功能加载的表格和工具自身参数。

部队训练
部队训练是一种游戏内活动，用于提升部队能力，但不会对副本内的状态产生实时影响。

英雄升级
英雄升级是一种游戏内活动，用于提升英雄属性或能力，但不会对副本内的状态产生实时影响。

科技研究
科技研究是一种游戏内活动，用于解锁或升级科技，但不会对副本内的状态产生实时影响。

技能升级
技能升级是一种游戏内活动，用于提升技能效果，但不会对副本内的状态产生实时影响。

GVG玩法配置表
GVG玩法配置表是存储GVG副本相关设置(如有效时间)的数据表格，用于游戏系统的配置。

玩家个人数据
玩家个人数据是记录玩家状态和进度的信息，在副本内通常不实时更新，需要额外开发沟通才能实现。

沙盘战报
沙盘战报是一种战报格式，GVG副本内战报的格式与此相同。

建筑
建筑是GVG副本中可以被争夺的目标，其战报会被缓存，供对战双方玩家查看。

我方联盟
我方联盟是GVG副本中对战的一方，在UI界面上其积分用蓝色文本框表示。

对方联盟
对方联盟是GVG副本中对战的另一方，在UI界面上其积分用红色文本框表示。

获胜联盟
获胜联盟是在GVG副本结束时积分更高的一方，在活动结束界面上其信息区域会显示“胜利”文本。

参战成员
参战成员是GVG副本中实际参与了战斗的联盟玩家，有资格获得特定的参战成员奖励。

未参战成员
未参战成员是GVG副本中属于参赛联盟但未实际参与战斗的玩家，有资格获得特定的未参战成员奖励。

条件
条件是关卡编辑器中的功能组件，可以使用黑板加载的配置表数据作为其参数。

触发器
触发器是关卡编辑器中的功能组件，可以使用黑板加载的配置表数据作为其参数。

GVG
GVG is a competitive event involving alliances, featuring stages like registration, matching, and a battle instance.

Matching Phase
The stage following registration where the system pairs alliances against each other as opponents.<SEP>The Matching Phase is a stage in the GVG event where the system pairs alliances against each other and sends notifications to players.

UI Interface
The UI Interface is the player-facing screen that displays information such as countdown timers, alliance details, and opponent information during the GVG event.<SEP>The user interface for the GvG instance activity, displaying opponent information and providing 'Enter' or 'Spectate' buttons based on the player's status.

Notification Email
An email sent to players to inform them about the status of the GVG activity, such as the start or end of the matching phase.<SEP>A Notification Email is sent to all alliance members each time a GVG registration or registration update is successfully completed, containing the selected session times and participant list.<SEP>Notification Emails are sent to players at the start and end of the Matching Phase to inform them about the status of opponent matching.<SEP>Messages sent to all players of both alliances to inform them of the GvG instance's opening and closing.

Alliance
A group of players that can register for the GVG activity, subject to conditions like server ranking or member count.<SEP>A group of players that can register and participate in the GVG activity.<SEP>An Alliance is a player organization that must meet specific conditions to be eligible to register for the GVG activity.<SEP>A social guild or group whose members can sign up for GVG activities and are organized into teams.<SEP>An Alliance is a player group that participates in GVG, with attributes like name, power, participant count, and server ID.<SEP>An Alliance is a player group that participates in the GVG instance, competing for control of buildings and accumulating points.<SEP>A group of players that participates in the GvG instance, with members either fighting or spectating based on the participation list.

Opponent Alliance
The Opponent Alliance is the matched rival in GVG, whose details are initially hidden and later revealed.

Battle Instance
The Battle Instance is the GVG combat stage that opens at a scheduled time, allowing players to enter and engage in battle.

Shield Effect
The Shield Effect is a protective status automatically applied to a player's main city in their home server upon first entering the GVG instance.

Entry Conditions
Entry Conditions are requirements a player must meet to enter the GVG battle instance, such as being selected and having no active troops.

Odd Alliance
An Odd Alliance is an alliance that remains unpaired after the matching process and is automatically marked as winning by default in the next stage.

Home Server
The Home Server is the player's original game server where their main city is located and where certain restrictions apply during GVG.

Player
An individual user who is a member of an alliance and can be selected for GVG activity participation.<SEP>An individual user who interacts with the GVG activity system, specifically the one who clicks the registration button and whose alliance and rank are checked for eligibility.<SEP>An individual participant who can be a member of an alliance and may enter the GVG instance.<SEP>A Player is a user who participates in the game, can be a member of an Alliance, and may be selected for the GVG activity registration list.<SEP>A Player is an individual participant who can be selected for GVG, must meet entry conditions, and can enter the battle instance.<SEP>A Player is an individual participant in the GVG instance who can attack buildings with personal or rallied troops.<SEP>A participant in the game who must meet specific conditions to enter the GvG instance and whose actions are restricted in the original server during the instance.

Bye Win
A rule where an alliance with no opponent after matching is automatically marked as the winner for that stage.<SEP>A Bye Win is the automatic victory granted to an Odd Alliance when it cannot be matched with an opponent.

Countdown Timer
The Countdown Timer is a UI element displayed centrally that shows the remaining time until matching completes or the battle instance opens.

Main City
The Main City is the player's primary settlement in their Home Server, which receives a Shield Effect during GVG and cannot be in combat for the player to enter the instance.<SEP>The player's primary settlement in the original server, which must not be in combat status and receives a shield when the player enters a GvG instance.

Enter Button
The Enter Button is an interactive UI element that players manually click to enter the GVG battle instance.

Help Text
Help Text is informational content displayed at the bottom center of the UI interface to guide players.

Exit Button
The Exit Button allows a player to leave the GVG battle instance and return to their Home Server.

Player City
A Player City is a player's settlement within the GVG battle instance, which remains attackable even if the player leaves and re-enters.

Troops
Troops are military units created by a player; having any troops prevents the player from entering the GVG battle instance.<SEP>Military units created by a player. A player cannot have any troops created to meet the entry condition for the GvG instance.

Inner City Hospital
The Inner City Hospital is a building where wounded soldiers are treated; it must contain no wounded soldiers for the player to enter GVG.<SEP>A building within the player's city that must not contain any wounded soldiers (either being treated or treatment completed) for the player to enter the GvG instance.

Game Main Interface
The Game Main Interface is the primary screen from which the GVG activity interface is accessed.

Wounded Soldiers
Wounded Soldiers are military units undergoing or finished with treatment in the Inner City Hospital; their presence blocks GVG entry.<SEP>Injured units within the Inner City Hospital. Their absence is a prerequisite for a player to enter the GvG instance.

GVG Activity Interface
The GVG Activity Interface displays the current activity stage name, the GVG activity instance's opening and closing times, and the time remaining until the next stage begins.

Registration Stage
The Registration Stage is a designated period during the GVG activity where eligible alliances can register themselves and selected members into the instance server matching queue for the next stage.

Registration Button
The Registration Button is a UI element within the activity interface that players click to initiate the registration process during the GVG Registration Stage.

Preview Stage
The Preview Stage is the initial phase of the GVG activity where the registration stage is not yet open, and the UI only shows the activity name and the time remaining until registration opens.

Battle Session
A Battle Session is a scheduled time slot for GVG instance battles, configured to accommodate global players across different UTC time zones.

Registration Details Interface
The Registration Details Interface is opened by clicking the registration button and allows players to select battle sessions and participating alliance members.

Matching System
The Matching System adjusts the final battle session time for an alliance based on the time slots and quantity selected during registration, according to predefined rules.

Registration List
The Registration List is the roster of all alliance members at the moment the alliance first submits its registration for the GVG activity.

Submit Registration Button
The Submit Registration Button is clicked by players to finalize and submit their GVG activity registration after selecting battle sessions and members.

Alliance Permission Table
The Alliance Permission Table configures which alliance ranks are granted the authority to perform GVG registration.

GVG Configuration Table
The GVG Configuration Table is a data file where project teams configure settings such as eligibility conditions, required alliance ranks, the number of battle sessions (M), and the maximum selectable sessions (N).

GVG Gameplay Rules Table
The GVG Gameplay Rules Table is a newly created configuration file that specifies the alliance ranks required for GVG registration.

UTC Time
UTC Time is the standard time reference used to schedule multiple Battle Sessions for the GVG activity to accommodate a global player base.

Level Editor File
A file that needs to be modified alongside the configuration table to change the maximum number of participants.<SEP>The Level Editor File is a configuration document that must be modified alongside the configuration table to increase the maximum number of registrants for the GVG activity.

GVG Activity
A competitive event for alliances that requires registration, has specific participation conditions, and involves multiple sessions.<SEP>A competitive event where alliances can sign up and participate, involving stages like registration and matching.<SEP>A series of structured events including announcement, sign-up, matching, instance opening, and results display for guild competition.<SEP>The GVG Activity is a recurring competitive event in the game involving alliances, with stages including Preview and Registration, leading to instance battles.

Framework Demo Version
A demonstration version of the system that uses simplified conditions for registration, such as "top 20 server ranking" or "more than 3 members".<SEP>The Framework Demo Version is a specific implementation of the GVG system where the registration cap is 5 players and member lists are not displayed in notification emails.

Play Configuration Table
A table used for data management between multiple triggers, loaded via the Blackboard.

Blackboard
A system within the Level Editor that loads configuration table data, making it visible in the editor tool interface for selection via dropdown lists.

Level Editor
A tool for designing and configuring the GVG Instance, including triggers, behaviors, and map elements.<SEP>A tool that includes configuration files for setting which tables are loaded for its various functions and its own parameters.

Editor Configuration File
A file added to the Level Editor to configure the tables loaded for editor functions and the tool's own parameters.

InstanceTag Table
A data table containing the tag selection rules for the GVG activity, which dictate how tags are chosen for participants in the framework demo.<SEP>A database table containing tag settings for GVG activities, referenced for tag selection rules.<SEP>A table that, based on the current instanceGroup id, loads relevant character IDs into the Level Editor's Blackboard for use as parameters in triggers and condition judgments.

InstanceGameConfig Table
A new table (not yet in the version) structured like a constant table, associating instanceGroup id with key-value pairs to provide values as trigger parameters in the Blackboard.

External Configuration Table
Refers to tables like instanceTag and instanceGameConfig that are loaded into the Blackboard from outside the editor.

Custom Variable
Variables defined within the Level Editor's Blackboard that can be used by triggers and conditions, and modified by trigger logic.

Trigger
A mechanism that initiates events, with specific types like Timer triggers mentioned.<SEP>Logic components in the Level Editor that use data from the Blackboard (configuration tables or custom variables) as parameters to execute events.

Condition Judgment
Components in the Level Editor that use data from the Blackboard (configuration tables or custom variables) as parameters to evaluate logic.

InstanceGroup Id
An identifier used to associate and load specific data from external configuration tables (like instanceTag and instanceGameConfig) into the Blackboard.

Timer Class Trigger
A specific type of trigger that fires an event every N seconds.<SEP>A type of trigger that fires an event every N seconds.

Seroli Instance Configuration
An example sheet/page within the instanceGameConfig table for configuring a specific gameplay instance.

GVG Instance Configuration
An example sheet/page within the instanceGameConfig table containing key-value constants for a specific gameplay mode, such as kill points and occupy points.

NodeCanvas
A framework component currently at a problematic version with memory leaks, requiring an upgrade to version 3.2.5.<SEP>A framework component in the project that currently has a memory leak issue and needs to be upgraded to version 3.2.5.

RpgRole Sheet
A specific sheet/page within the instanceTag table that is registered in the editor configuration, with specific fields marked as loadable into the Blackboard.

Constant Table
A referenced table structure that the new instanceGameConfig table is designed to be similar to, using an integer id and a key-value association system.

Sheet Name
Part of a proposed unique identifier (e.g., sheet name + field name) for fields loaded from external configuration tables into the Blackboard.

Field Name
Part of a proposed unique identifier (e.g., sheet name + field name) for fields loaded from external configuration tables into the Blackboard.

data.entity_name: Trigger Logic And Condition Configuration Method Adjustment
data.content:
Trigger Logic And Condition Configuration Method Adjustment
A planned adjustment to classify and abstract triggers and conditions by event type, managed via configuration files.

NodeCanvas Version 3.2.5
The target version (3.2.5) to upgrade the NodeCanvas framework to, in order to resolve a memory leak issue.

UI
The user interface for the GVG activity sign-up and management system, displaying player lists and controls.

Matching Server
A server responsible for executing the matching logic to find suitable GVG opponents for alliances.

Registration Phase
The initial stage of the GVG activity where alliances submit their participant lists.

Matching Rules
The configured logic, such as pairing alliances with the closest total combat power, used to determine GVG matchups.

Sign-Up Button
A UI button with text that changes to "Update Sign-Up" for modifying an existing registration.<SEP>A button in the GVG activity registration interface that, when clicked, triggers a check of the player's alliance eligibility and rank permissions before opening the registration details interface.

Total Alliance Combat Power
A key matching data point calculated as the sum of the personal combat power of all members in the sign-up list.

Match Queue
A processing queue for a specific GVG session where alliances are sorted and paired during the matching phase.

System Prompt
A message displayed to the user, such as "XXX has left the alliance, please reselect members," when a submission fails due to a player leaving.

Personal Combat Power
An individual player's power rating, which is summed with others to calculate the Total Alliance Combat Power.

GVG Interface
The specific screen or view that players access through the activity interface to see GVG-related information.

Sign-Up List
The list of players selected by an alliance to participate in the GVG activity, managed through the UI.

Tag
A label or category that can be assigned to participants in the GVG activity, with selection rules defined in the InstanceTag table.<SEP>A label or identifier used within the GVG activity system, with selection rules defined in the InstanceTag table.

Session
A specific time slot or instance of the GVG activity for which an alliance can register.

Event Classification And Abstraction
A method for categorizing and abstracting events, to be implemented via configuration files for addition, deletion, and modification.

Configuration File
A file used to manage event logic by adding, deleting, or modifying configurations.

Brush
A tool within the Map Editor for painting on the grid, with configurable size and color.

Walkable Area
An area on the map designated as passable, with a customizable color.

Blackboard Function
A pending system feature that will determine the necessity of supplementing trigger requirements.

Blocking Area
An area on the map designated as impassable, with a customizable color.

Grid Information
The tile data on the scene that correctly displays the effects of the brush.

User Experience (UE) Correction
An adjustment to highlight all selection-type controls (tabs or buttons) on the interface to indicate their selected state.

Map Editor
A tool for editing and placing world buildings within the GVG Instance scene.<SEP>A tool used to place world buildings like the One-Way Air Wall into the GvG instance scenario.<SEP>A tool for editing maps, with specific brush and placement functionalities.

Neutral Building
A building in the GvG instance that is initially unclaimed and can be captured by either alliance, synonymous with Contestable Building.<SEP>A type of object that can be edited and placed within the Map Editor.

Memory Leak
A problem affecting the current version of NodeCanvas, significantly impacting development use.

Brush Size
The configurable side length of the square brush, with a default value of 1.

Brush Color
The configurable color property of the brush for setting blocking and walkable areas.

Selection-Type Control
Interface elements like tabs or buttons that require visual highlighting to indicate a selected state.

Tool Requirement
A specification for the Map Editor indicating it is a development tool that does not require testing validation.

Version 3.2.5
The latest version of NodeCanvas to which the framework needs to be upgraded.

Neutral Buildings
Neutral Buildings are structures placed in the GVG instance battlefield that can be contested by players from both alliances.

GVG Instance Total Points
GVG Instance Total Points is the cumulative score for an alliance within the GVG instance, used to determine victory.

City Teleport
City Teleport is a function allowing players to relocate their city within the instance using either a fixed-point or random method.

Hospital Building
The Hospital Building is a structure within a player's city in the instance that provides enhanced healing functions.

Level Editor Blackboard Function
The Level Editor Blackboard Function is a tool used to schedule and configure the points and intervals for buildings in the instance.

Battle Report
A Battle Report is a log of combat events that players can view by clicking on a building in the instance.

Instance Valid Time
Instance Valid Time is the predefined duration from the opening to the closing of the GVG instance, displayed as a countdown.

data_constant.xlsx
data_constant.xlsx is a configuration file containing a temporary setting for building occupation points under the constant 'gvg_building_score'.

Instance Points
Instance Points are the sole criterion for determining the winning alliance in a GVG instance, accumulated through various actions.<SEP>Points earned by an alliance in the GvG instance through actions like capturing buildings or defeating enemy player troops, contributing to the total score.

Garrison Troops
Garrison Troops are defensive military units stationed in a Neutral Building by the occupying alliance.

Personal Troops
Personal Troops are the individual military units belonging to a single player, used to attack buildings.

Rallied Troops
Rallied Troops are combined military forces from multiple players used to attack a Neutral Building.

Player Personal Data Snapshot
Player Personal Data Snapshot is a record of a player's state (e.g., troops, research) captured upon first entering the instance.

Fixed-Point Teleport
Fixed-Point Teleport is a type of City Teleport that allows a player to choose a specific location for relocation.

Random Teleport
Random Teleport is a type of City Teleport that relocates a player's city to a random location within the instance.

Acceleration Items
Acceleration Items are consumable items from a player's backpack that can be used to speed up healing in the instance hospital.

Spawn Area
Designated regions on the GvG instance map (e.g., top-left, bottom-right) where players from each alliance initially place their cities upon entry.<SEP>A Spawn Area is the initial region where an alliance's players begin within the GVG instance.

GvG Instance
A competitive event where two alliances battle in a separate scenario, with specific entry conditions, rules, and objectives like capturing buildings and earning points.

Spawn Point
Specific locations within a Spawn Area where an entering player's city is created, with a quantity matching the maximum team size.

Contestable Building
A neutral structure in the GvG instance that alliances can capture to earn periodic points and a one-time reward upon capture.

Original Server
The main game server where a player's primary city is located, which gains a protective shield and restricts troop deployment when the player enters a GvG instance.

One-Way Air Wall
A world building placed in the GvG instance that allows only players from one alliance to pass through it once, used to separate spawn areas from combat zones.

Spawn Point Trigger
A game mechanism associated with spawn points that needs to support the setting of ownership.

Protective Shield
An effect automatically applied to a player's main city in the original server when they first enter a GvG instance, lasting until that instance ends.

City Relocation Item
An item that allows a player to move their city within the GvG instance.

Battle Status
A condition where a player's main city is engaged in combat, which must not be true for the player to enter the GvG instance.

Participation List
A list of players selected to participate in the GvG instance, determining whether a player can enter or only spectate.

Entry Restriction Check
A verification process performed when a player on the participation list attempts to enter the instance, checking for troops and wounded soldiers.

Spectate
The action available to alliance members not on the participation list, allowing them to view the GvG instance without participating.

Point Refresh
The mechanism of updating the scores of both alliances displayed on the UI interface each time it is opened.

World Building
A general category for structures placed in the game world, such as the One-Way Air Wall, using the Map Editor.

Garrisoned Building
A type of building that can be attacked and defended, used to verify combat mechanics within the GvG instance.

Reward Email
An email sent to players after the GvG instance ends, allowing them to check their rewards.

Registration Notification Email
An email sent to all alliance members upon successful registration or registration update, containing the selected session times and the list of participating members.

Configuration Table
A data file that needs to be modified alongside the level editor file to change the maximum number of participants.

Session List
A list of available time slots for the GVG activity, created based on the total number of configured sessions, each with a checkbox for selection.

Participant List
A list template based on the alliance member list, used to select participants and their tags for the GVG activity, with checkboxes for participation and tags.

Checkbox
An interactive UI element used in the session list and participant list for selecting event sessions and marking participant status/tags.

GVG Activity Registration Interface
A user interface for registering for the GVG activity, displaying the activity name, remaining registration time, a sign-up button, session lists, and participant lists.

UI Template
A user interface template that project teams are to implement themselves, used for displaying information like member lists in emails or the registration interface.

GVG Framework
A framework for organizing and executing GVG (Guild vs. Guild) activities, involving development and functional implementation.

Activity System
A system that manages the scheduling, configuration, and progression of game activities, including GVG events.

activity.xlsx
A configuration spreadsheet used to define and link the sequential stages of the GVG activity series.

Strongest Commander Activity
An existing game activity series used as a reference model for configuring the GVG activity series.

GVG Announcement Activity
The initial phase of the GVG series where the upcoming event is previewed to all server players.

GVG Matching Result Publicity
The phase where the system's matchmaking results are displayed to the participating alliances.

GVG Instance Opening
The phase where the competitive instance opens, allowing matched alliance players to enter.

GVG Sign-up Activity
The phase where eligible alliances register for the GVG competition and select match time slots.

GVG Results Display
The final phase of the series where the outcomes and rewards of the GVG instance are shown.

Alliance Server
A server handling social and guild-related data and functions for the GVG activity.

Instance Server
A server dedicated to running the GVG combat instance and its internal logic.

Game Server
The main game server where players normally reside, interfacing with the activity and instance systems.

GVG Announcement Interface
A specific UI screen that displays information about the upcoming GVG activity to players.

GVG Instance
A competitive battlefield where matched alliances engage in gameplay such as building capture and PvP combat.

UI (User Interface)
The graphical interface through which players interact with the GVG activity, including announcement and sign-up screens.

Spectator Function
A feature allowing non-participating players to observe the ongoing battle within the GVG Instance.

Battlefield LOD Function
A technical feature using color blocks to represent the states of capturable buildings in the GVG Instance for testing.

Combat Report
A report generated from battles within the GVG Instance, viewable in relation to specific captured buildings.

Instance Settlement and Rewards
The concluding phase of the GVG Instance where scores are calculated, rankings are determined, and rewards are distributed.

Instance Server Blackboard
A functionality within the Instance Server for managing shared state or data during the GVG Instance.

Vision And Goals
A section on the home page describing the objectives and significance of building the knowledge space to align team efforts.

Common Documents And Links
A section for listing important document links to facilitate quick access for members.

Knowledge Space
A knowledge space is a structured environment for organizing and sharing information among team members.

Home Page
The home page is the main entry point of a knowledge space, providing an overview and key sections for users.

Knowledge Space Help
A section containing usage guidelines, operational procedures, and administrator contact information for the knowledge base.

Team Members
Team members are the intended audience and users of the knowledge space, working towards its goals.

Knowledge Space Introduction
A section introducing the main content and target audience of the knowledge space.
